compiled_state.title: As a validation process, I can capture implementation screenshots with stabilization
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Playwright captures screenshots of implemented routes at a configurable viewport with stabilization (networkidle + settle delay + animation disabling) for reliable perceptual comparison.
**Why**: Provides the implementation image that the perceptual diff compares against the Figma design export.
**Scope**:
- IN: Playwright screenshot capture at configured viewport (design.figma_viewport, default 1440x900), networkidle wait + 500ms settle delay, CSS animation disabling during capture, output path convention matching S1 for S3 pairing
- OUT: Figma export (S1), perceptual diff scoring (S3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, Playwright captures screenshots at the configured viewport dimensions (design.figma_viewport, default 1440x900) with networkidle wait state + 500ms settle delay to avoid false failures from animations or lazy-loaded content
  ← Satisfies: "captures Playwright screenshots with stabilization"
- When this story is complete, CSS animations are disabled during capture to prevent non-deterministic screenshot content
  ← Satisfies: "explicit wait conditions to avoid false failures"
- When this story is complete, screenshots are saved to designs/{uuid}/validation/<route_identifier>.png with a documented naming convention that S3 can use to pair with Figma exports
  ← Satisfies: adversarial review finding on output path contract
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Font rendering varies across CI environments — document expected variance and its impact on fidelity scores
- [Architecture] design-wireframe already captures screenshots to designs/{uuid}/screenshots/. Evaluate reusing that pipeline or document why separate capture is needed (different viewport, animation disabling, stabilization requirements for comparison)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
